name: Riot.js
description: Simple and elegant component-based UI library with custom elements

description.value: Simple and elegant component-based UI library with custom elements

initial-release.comment: Created by Muut in November 2013, shortly after React was publicly released

active-maintenance.comment: Multiple releases in 2024 including major v10.0.0 and patch releases

reactivity-model.comment: Uses manual update model with this.update() and props/state. Pre-compiled expressions for minimal DOM updates

component-model.comment: Single-file .riot components combining HTML layout and JavaScript logic

rendering-paradigm.comment: Declarative templates with curly brace expressions

state-management-approach.comment: Built-in this.state for local component state with this.update() for triggering re-renders

opinionatedness.comment: Described as 'open stack' allowing developers to mix and match design patterns

backward-compatibility.comment: v10.0.0 introduced breaking changes; major versions have brought significant API changes

typescript-support.comment: Compiler supports TypeScript syntax. Official examples available but not primary focus

template-syntax.comment: .riot files with curly brace expressions {expression} and three directives: if, each, is

styling-approach.comment: Supports scoped CSS within .riot component files

build-required.comment: Available via CDN (unpkg, jsDelivr, cdnjs) for no-build usage; CLI compilation recommended for production

reactive-primitives.comment: No signals/refs; uses this.state object with manual this.update() calls

computed-values.comment: No built-in computed/derived state; expressions are pre-compiled but not reactive

two-way-binding.comment: One-way data flow; two-way binding must be implemented manually

event-handling.comment: Declarative on* attributes with automatic this binding

context-provide-inject.comment: No built-in dependency injection; props flow from parent to child

refs-direct-dom-access.comment: Direct DOM manipulation supported; no virtual DOM

nested-routes.comment: Not documented; minimal router focused on core URL matching

dynamic-routes.comment: Supports path patterns with parameters like /:some/:route/:param

lazy-loading-routes.comment: No code-splitting or async route loading patterns shown

scroll-restoration.comment: No automatic scroll position management documented

built-in-global-state.comment: No official global state solution; component state only

devtools-state-inspection.comment: No official devtools extension; community project riot-devtools exists but not widely adopted

time-travel-debugging.comment: No official devtools support for time-travel debugging

browser-devtools.comment: No official browser extension; community discussed but never officially developed

vs-code-extension.comment: JetBrains plugin exists; no official or widely-used VS Code extension found

error-messages.comment: Chrome recognizes source locations; other browsers may show generic references

documentation-quality.comment: Adequate documentation with examples and API reference; fewer guides than major frameworks

bundle-size-core.comment: 6.1 KB gzipped as stated on official website

bundle-size-full.comment: Estimated ~10 KB with router; modular ecosystem

virtual-dom.comment: No virtual DOM; direct DOM manipulation with minimal updates

tree-shaking.comment: Modular ecosystem designed for tree shaking

code-splitting.comment: Supports code splitting via bundlers (Webpack, Rollup, Parcel)

streaming-ssr.comment: @riotjs/ssr renders to HTML strings, no streaming support documented

partial-hydration.comment: No islands architecture or partial hydration documented

js-framework-benchmark.comment: Historically performed poorly in benchmarks; current v10 scores not readily available

component-libraries.comment: Very limited; no major UI component libraries compared to larger frameworks

major-companies-using.comment: 2dehands.be has 12M+ monthly visits; originally created by Muut

official-sponsor-backer.comment: Community-driven; originally created by Muut, now maintained by Gianluca Guarini and contributors

discord-community-size.comment: No dedicated Riot.js Discord found; uses GitHub discussions and old Muut forums

stack-overflow-questions.comment: Unable to retrieve exact count; relatively low activity compared to major frameworks

job-market-demand.comment: Niche; very few job postings specifically for Riot.js

testing-library-name.comment: Community library; @riotjs/ssr can also be used for rendering tests

e2e-testing-integration.comment: Standard E2E tools (Playwright, Cypress) work with Riot apps

snapshot-testing.comment: Possible via @riotjs/ssr string output or riot-test-utils

pwa-support.comment: No built-in PWA tooling; standard web PWA techniques apply

desktop-framework.comment: No official desktop framework; can be used with Electron

learning-curve.comment: Only 3 template directives (if, each, is); simple API designed for quick adoption

concepts-to-learn.comment: Components, props/state, lifecycle methods, template directives (if/each/is), update mechanism

time-to-hello-world.comment: ~5 minutes; can use CDN without build step for quick start

migration-path.comment: No official migration guides from other frameworks

incremental-adoption.comment: Embeddable; can mount components to specific DOM elements in existing apps
